title: Timer: starts on first two rows after resync
description: When view re-renders with active timer, resync code matches multiple rows with same task text. Also when starting timer on a new row, previous row display doesn't update.

title: Filter engine: no effect when applied
description:
Clicking Filter → Apply in toolbar has no effect on table rows. Likely:
1. Filter is stored on renderer instance but loadTasks() doesn't check it correctly.
2. Or filter value types mismatch (string vs number).
3. Need to verify filter application in loadTasks().

title: Session storage: t.split error and ENOTDIR
description:
1. t.split is not a function: startTime is Date object not string in onSessionEnd. 
2. ENOTDIR: flowtime/sessions/ path blocked by existing 'flowtime' file.
3. vault.adapter.append/write may fail silently.
Fix: convert startTime to ISO string, remove blocking file, add better error handling.

title: Bucket defaults not showing in settings
description: 3 default buckets (Deep Work 20h, Admin 5h, Meetings 5h) don't appear in settings. Likely because existing saved data.json doesn't have buckets key, and Object.assign merges incorrectly. Need to ensure defaults propagate.

title: Timer sync: table ↔ status bar pause/resume/stop out of sync
description:
1. Pausing from table then resuming creates phantom session and resets status timer instead of syncing remaining time. 
2. Right-click stop on status bar does not stop table timer.
3. Per-row timer and status timer run independent intervals — toggle should use statusTimer.toggle() not stop()+start()

title: Progress bar component
description: Build progress bar component. Visual bar showing used/remaining for any budget (bucket weekly limit, daily cap). JS computes ratio + sets inline width + state class. CSS renders bar with color transitions. Text overlay '14.2h / 20h' for accessibility. Reference: plan_v2.md P2.
acceptance_criteria: Progress bar renders correctly with computed width. Text overlay shows used/total. State class switches correctly on threshold boundaries.

title: 3-state color system constants and CSS
description: Define 3-state system: Normal (0-79%) = neutral/default color, Warning (80-99%) = amber, Over (100%+) = red. Add CSS classes for each state. Provide JS helper getBudgetState(used, total) returning {ratio, state}. Hardcoded thresholds, document rationale. Reference: plan_v2.md P1.
acceptance_criteria: 3 CSS classes exist (ft-state-normal, ft-state-warning, ft-state-over). Helper function getBudgetState(used, total) returns correct state and ratio.

title: Weekly limit tracking per bucket
description: Calculate total scheduled time per bucket for the current week (sum of @<duration> on tasks for this week). Compare against bucket's weekly limit. Show remaining budget in bucket column or tooltip. Uses 3-state color system. Week rollover resets naturally (old data queryable but doesn't count). Reference: plan_v2.md B3.
acceptance_criteria: Bucket column shows used/remaining time for the week. Exceeding weekly limit shows over-budget state via 3-state color.

title: Bucket assignment in quick entry + table
description: Add bucket column to all table views. Quick entry modal gets bucket dropdown (auto-suggest from available buckets). Saves as @bucket:<name> or @b:<name> on task line. Click bucket badge in table to reassign. Bidirectional: text field source of truth, helper fields write @directives into text. Reference: plan_v2.md B2.
acceptance_criteria: Bucket column shows in all table views. Quick entry has bucket dropdown. Assigning/reassigning bucket updates the @bucket: or @b: directive on the task line.

title: Bucket CRUD — create/edit/delete bucket categories
description: Build bucket data model. Each bucket has: name, emoji/color, weekly limit (hours, > 0 validated), sort order. Settings tab section to manage buckets (add/edit/delete/reorder). Store in plugin settings. Default prefix: 'budget/' (configurable). Reference: plan_v2.md B1.
acceptance_criteria: Users can create, edit, and delete buckets in settings. Buckets persist across restarts. Each bucket has name, color, weekly limit. Validation rejects 0 or negative limits.

title: Progress Bars + 3-State Color System
description: Visual budget feedback system. Normal (0-79%) = neutral, Warning (80-99%) = amber, Over (100%+) = red. Hardcoded thresholds, documented rationale. JS compute ratio + state class, CSS renders. Text overlay '14.2h / 20h' for accessibility. Apply to bucket weekly limits, daily cap, and timer countdown.
acceptance_criteria: All budget displays show color-state progress bars. Normal/warning/over states are visually distinct. Progress bars have text overlay labels. Timer shows progress toward duration.

comments.author: Radialarray
comments.text: Review decisions: JS compute + CSS render. Text labels on bars for accessibility. Thresholds hardcoded (0-79%/80-99%/100%+). Progress bars use inline width + state class from JS.

title: Buckets System — Time Budget Categories
description: Buckets are time-constrained categories (e.g. 'Deep Work - 20hrs/week'). Every task MUST belong to a bucket. Orthogonal to projects. Includes: bucket CRUD (validate >0 weekly limit), bucket assignment in quick entry + table (@bucket:name or @b:name syntax), weekly limit tracking, daily budget cap (default 12h, scheduled time tracking only). Default bucket prefix: 'budget/' (configurable). Migration prompt on first v2 load.
acceptance_criteria: Buckets are configurable via settings tab. Tasks can be assigned to buckets. Weekly limits show remaining budget. Daily cap warns when approaching limit.

comments.author: Radialarray
comments.text: Review decisions: bucket prefix defaults to 'budget/' (not 'bucket/'). Migration prompt on first load for unassigned tasks. Daily cap tracks scheduled time only (sum of @<duration>). Bidirectional quick entry — text field source of truth, helpers write @directives. Inline indicator for daily cap warning. Default templates include bucket-summary section.

title: Sprint 3: Quick entry modal — natural date parsing + task capture
description: Build natural language date parser: @today, @tomorrow, @next-monday, @fri, @in-3-days, @2026-12-01. Create modal UI with task text, date (natural language), project suggester (auto-detect from active file), duration dropdown. Register command palette entry 'Flowtime: Add Task'. Insert formatted task into target file. Depends on sprint 1 (foundation).
acceptance_criteria: Cmd+P > 'Flowtime: Add Task' opens modal. Entering task + date + project creates correct task line. Date parsing handles all listed formats.

comments.author: Radialarray
comments.text: Created src/date-parser.js (all formats, 52/52 tests pass), src/quick-entry.js (modal with live preview + Enter submit), modified main.js (command registration), modified styles.css (modal CSS).

title: Sprint 2: Project detection engine — folder traversal + frontmatter parsing
description: Implement folder traversal upward from task file to find folder note with frontmatter marker (type: project). Build project cache with invalidation on file save. Add project column to all table renderers. Resolve #project/tag from task lines.
acceptance_criteria: Project column appears in all three table modes. Tasks under project folders show correct project name. Configured frontmatter key works.

title: Sprint 1: Plugin Foundation — rename, modularize, settings tab
description: Rename plugin from flowtime to flowtime. Split main.js into src/ modules (project-engine, task-parser, date-parser, quick-entry, status-timer, renderer, settings). Add settings tab with all config options. Update manifest.json.
acceptance_criteria: Plugin loads as 'Flowtime' in Obsidian. Settings tab shows all options. Existing code blocks still work.

title: Create RoutineEngine class
description: New src/routine-engine.js with: scanRoutinesFolder() to read template files, generateDueTasks() to evaluate and write instances, tracking via .generated.json. Uses the recurrence parser to determine if each task line is due.

title: Extend parseRecurrence with day-name support
description: Add parsing for markers like 'every Mon', 'every Mon Wed Fri', 'every Sun'. Internal representation should capture day names as an array and the recurrence engine evaluates 'does today match any of these days?'

title: Create weekplan-renderer.js
description: New src/weekplan-renderer.js. Renders Monday-Friday day-by-day list. Loads tasks for each day via _getFileTasks, combines with routine instances, displays with time, duration, checkbox, edit controls. Implements MarkdownRenderChild pattern.

title: Weekplan inline editing and toolbar actions
description: Implement: task checkboxes (write to source file), delete button (remove instance + mark in .generated.json), inline timer, time/duration dropdowns, daily budget progress bars, regenerate button, vacation mode toggle, Add Task quick entry.

title: Register flowtime-weekplan code block mode
description: In renderer.js, register 'weekplan' mode alongside today/overdue/weekly/etc. Route to WeekplanRenderer when mode is 'weekplan'. Hook into the existing code block handler.

title: Add workday recurrence mode
description: Parse 'every workday' marker. Workdays defined by settings (default Mon-Fri). Engine evaluates true if today is a configured workday.

title: Implement .generated.json tracking
description: Read/write metadata file at flowtime/routines/.generated.json. Schema: entries with routineFile, lineHash, targetDate, generatedAt. On generation, hash the task line content and check if an entry already exists for that line+date combo.

title: Wire RoutineEngine into plugin lifecycle
description: Hook RoutineEngine into Plugin.onload() (auto-generate on startup if setting enabled), register command palette entry for manual generation, watch routine file saves (debounced re-evaluate).

title: Routine Engine
description: New RoutineEngine module that scans the routines folder, parses template task lines with recurrence markers, evaluates what's due, writes instances into daily notes, and tracks generation history in flowtime/routines/.generated.json to prevent cross-device duplication.

title: Weekplan View — Day-by-Day List
description: New flowtime-weekplan code block that renders Monday-Friday with all tasks (routines + one-offs) in a day-by-day list layout. Includes inline editing (checkboxes, delete, timer, time/duration dropdowns), daily budget bars, regenerate routines button, vacation mode quick toggle, and Add Task button.

title: Recurrence Parser
description: Extend the existing parseRecurrence() function to handle all planned interval types: day names (Mon, Tue, etc.), workday support, nth-day-of-month, multi-day patterns, and custom intervals like 'every 3 days'. This is the foundation that everything else builds on.

title: ProcessInboxModal + Process Inbox command
description: Create ProcessInboxModal class in src/inbox-processor.js. One-at-a-time line processing: editable text, action dropdown (Task/Project/Wiki/Discard/Snooze), conditional fields per action. Pre-fill from existing tags. Task output uses quickEntryTargetFile setting. Project scaffold uses TemplateEngine. Wiki appends to project wiki. On confirm: apply action, remove line from inbox list. On exit: write remaining lines back. Register 'Process Inbox' command in main.js. Add 'Snooze' support — append @snooze YYYY-MM-DD to line.
acceptance_criteria: Process Inbox command is registered. Modal opens with first unprocessed line. Each action shows correct fields. Pre-fill works from existing tags. Process removes line from list. Discard removes without creating anything. Project scaffolds via TemplateEngine. Wiki appends to project wiki. Snooze appends @snooze date. Done writes remaining lines back. Summary notification after processing.
close_reason: ProcessInboxModal with 5 actions (Task/Project/Wiki/Discard/Snooze), conditional fields, Process Inbox command registered

title: InboxProcessor module
description: Create src/inbox-processor.js with an InboxProcessor class. Methods: readInbox() — reads the inbox file and returns array of processable lines (non-blank, non-heading). writeInbox(lines) — writes back remaining lines after processing. Lines carry metadata (original text, line index, detected tags from task-parser). Also filter out snoozed lines whose @snooze date hasn't arrived yet.
acceptance_criteria: readInbox() returns only non-blank, non-heading lines. writeInbox() persists remaining lines correctly. Snoozed lines filtered until their date passes. Detected tags (date, duration, bucket, project) are extracted for pre-fill.
close_reason: InboxProcessor module: parseInbox, reconstructInbox, detectTags, cleanDescription, buildTaskLine, isSnoozed, InboxItem class

title: Capture methods
description: Implement three ways to capture into the inbox: (1) @inbox command macro in AtCompletionsSuggest — expands to '- [ ] ' on blank line. (2) 'Append to Inbox' command — opens a simple text prompt modal, appends line to Inbox.md. (3) Quick Entry inbox target — add 'inbox' as valid quickEntryTargetFile value; when selected, QuickEntryModal writes to Inbox.md instead of daily note.
acceptance_criteria: @inbox macro works in command mode on blank line. 'Append to Inbox' command opens a text prompt, appends line to Inbox.md. Quick Entry with inbox target writes to Inbox.md.
close_reason: @inbox macro in AtCompletionsSuggest, Append to Inbox command with textarea modal, Quick Entry inbox target handler

title: Settings + auto-create for inbox
description: Add inboxPath setting to DEFAULT_SETTINGS with default 'Inbox.md'. Add inboxPath field to FlowtimeSettingsTab. On plugin load, check if inboxPath file exists and create it with template if not. Register modified settings on save.
acceptance_criteria: inboxPath setting exists in data.json with default 'Inbox.md'. Settings tab shows inboxPath input field. On plugin load, if Inbox.md doesn't exist, it's created with header + instructions. Changing inboxPath works on next load.
close_reason: Settings (inboxPath, defaults, settings tab), auto-create Inbox.md, Quick Entry inbox target added

title: Inbox Capture & Processing
description: Add an inbox feature to Flowtime: a simple Inbox.md file at vault root that accepts raw task lines, and a processing command to transform them into proper Flowtime entities (tasks, projects, wiki notes).
acceptance_criteria: Inbox.md is auto-created on plugin load. User can dump raw lines into Inbox.md with no syntax required. User can append lines to inbox via a command. User can set quick entry target to inbox. @inbox macro available in AtCompletionsSuggest. Process Inbox command opens a modal that walks through unprocessed lines one at a time. Actions: Task, Project, Wiki, Discard, Snooze. Processed lines are removed from inbox. Tags in line pre-fill processing fields. Styling consistent with existing Flowtime modals. Edge cases handled: empty inbox, missing inbox, concurrent edits.
close_reason: All 6 child tasks complete. Full inbox implementation: Inbox.md auto-create, capture methods (@inbox macro, Append command, Quick Entry target), InboxProcessor module, ProcessInboxModal with 5 actions, styling, edge cases.

title: Dashboard creation methods (Dashboard.md + Dashboard Weekly.md)
description: problems.md #1 — Template engine methods for daily and weekly dashboards with code blocks.
close_reason: Done — getDashboardDailyTemplate, getDashboardWeeklyTemplate, createDashboard() in template-engine.js

title: Multi-step onboarding wizard (Steps 1-5)
description: problems.md #1 — Layout → dashboards → buckets → daily notes → first project. Uses template-engine dashboard creation methods.
close_reason: Done — 5-step wizard in onboard.js: layout, dashboards, buckets, daily notes, first project

title: Session dir auto-creation + NDJSON validation
description: problems.md #5, #10.4 — Auto-create flowtime/sessions/ on plugin load, validate session/completion records before writing
close_reason: Done — session dir auto-created on load, NDJSON validation for writeSession/writeCompletion

title: Move _taskCache to separate file + auto-eviction + safety limits
description: problems.md #2, #10.1, #10.5 — Separate task-cache.json, auto-evict stale entries, safety limits at 5000 entries/1MB, skip files outside projectsRoot, skip empty entries
close_reason: Done — separate task-cache.json, auto-eviction, safety limits, respects projectsRoot, no empty entries

title: Sort indicator not showing on new columns
description: Sort direction ▲▼ doesn't show on columns clicked for the first time. Only after re-clicking the same column. Likely initial _sortConfig doesn't set field name correctly for header indicator lookup.

title: Migration prompt on first v2 load
description: On first v2 load, prompt user to pick or create a default bucket. Scan vault for tasks without @bucket: or @b: directive. Auto-tag them with @bucket:<default>. One-time migration, runs only once.
acceptance_criteria: Migration prompt shows on first v2 load. User picks or creates a bucket. Unassigned tasks get @bucket:<default> directive.

title: Set up Bun unit tests + clean up test artifacts
description: Set up Bun test suite for pure-logic modules (task-parser, date-parser, session-store, budget-state, cache). Remove dead test.html file. Tests in test/unit/<module>.test.js. Script: 'bun test test/unit'. Keep test.sh for Obsidian integration tests.
acceptance_criteria: Bun test suite runs and passes for all pure-logic modules. Dead test.html removed or repurposed. Integration tests still pass.
close_reason: Completed — test infrastructure was set up

title: Persistent task cache + incremental invalidation
description: Build persistent task cache. In-memory Map<filePath, TaskLine[]> on plugin instance. Written to cache file in plugin data directory (survives restart). Invalidation: file save/modify -> re-parse that file, Flowtime actions -> re-parse affected file(s). On render -> read from cache, no vault scan.
acceptance_criteria: Cache survives restart. Renders read from cache. File save/modify re-parses only that file. Cache file written correctly.

title: New @-prefix task syntax parser
description:
Redesign task parser to handle new @-prefix syntax (replaces emoji format). Implement in src/task-parser.js. Parse rules:
- @<value> where value contains colon (no 'due') = start time
- @<value> where value ends in 'h' or 'm' = duration
- @<value> where value starts with 'every-' = recurrence
- @<value> where value starts with 'due:' = due date
- @<value> where value starts with 'bucket:' or 'b:' = bucket
- @<value> where value starts with 'project:' or 'p:' = project
- otherwise @<value> = scheduled date
No backward compat with old emoji syntax (⏳, 📅, 🔁, 🔺, time blocks).
acceptance_criteria: All @-directive types parsed correctly. Old emoji format not supported. Line construction works.
close_reason: Completed — new @-prefix parser was already implemented

title: v0.3.0 Foundation — syntax redesign, cache, testing, migration
description:
Infrastructure changes spanning all v0.3.0 features. Breaking change from v0.2.0.

1. New @-prefix task syntax (replaces emoji format)
2. Persistent task cache + incremental parsing (Map<filePath, TaskLine[]>, written to cache file)
3. Two-tier testing setup (Bun unit tests + integration tests via test.sh)
4. Migration prompt on first v2 load (assign default bucket to unassigned tasks)
5. Version bump to 0.3.0 in manifest.json
6. Remove old emoji parsing from task parser
acceptance_criteria: New @-syntax parsing works for all directive types. Cache survives restart. Bun test suite exists and runs. Migration prompt shows on first load. manifest.json bumped to 0.3.0.
close_reason: Epic completed — all v0.3.0 subtasks done

title: Session history view — browse past sessions
description: New code block or modal to browse past sessions with date/bucket filters. Show sessions grouped by day or bucket. Support date range and bucket filter queries. Reference: plan_v2.md S2.
acceptance_criteria: Session history is browsable. Filters work. Each session shows task name, duration, date, bucket, notes.

title: Session storage format — persist timer sessions
acceptance_criteria: Timers write session records on stop/expiry. Checkbox toggles write completion records. NDJSON format correct. Files survive restart and sync.

title: Timer budget bar
description: Timer display shows progress bar toward scheduled duration, colored by state. Progress decreases as timer counts down. Reference: plan_v2.md P3.
acceptance_criteria: Timer shows progress bar. Bar is neutral during first 80%, amber 80-99%, red when over. Timer expiry still triggers notification.

title: Daily budget cap
description: Add configurable daily budget cap (default 12h, scheduled time only). In today view, show aggregate of all scheduled task durations (sum of @<duration>). If approaching or exceeding cap, show inline indicator in daily cap summary row using 3-state color system. Reference: plan_v2.md B4.
acceptance_criteria: Daily cap is configurable in settings. Today view shows total scheduled time vs cap. Inline warning shows when approaching limit (amber) and over (red).

title: Session Persistence & Analytics
description: Timer sessions are currently ephemeral (in-memory). Persist sessions to vault files so they survive Obsidian restart. Storage: NDJSON append-only in flowtime/sessions/<date>.ndjson. Per bucket, per session, per day, per week tracking (not per task). Includes completion records. Session history view, time analytics. Cross-device sync via Obsidian Sync.
acceptance_criteria: Timer sessions persist across Obsidian restarts. Session history view shows past sessions with date/bucket filters. Analytics show time-by-bucket (per day, per week). Last N completions queryable.

comments.author: Radialarray
comments.text: Review decisions: NDJSON format in flowtime/sessions/. Per bucket/session/day/week tracking (not per task). Completion records for done task count. Timer completion hook added via status-timer.js extraction (ships in Quick Wins). Session record: {type, date, start_time, end_time, duration_minutes, bucket, task_text, notes}. Completion record: {type, date, bucket, task_text, completed_at}. Analytics: time-by-bucket per day/per week, last N completions.

title: Sprint 5b: Template engine — dashboard insertion + new project command
description: Build template engine module (src/template-engine.js). Load templates from settings or defaults. Support variables: {{DATE}}, {{WEEK_START}}, {{WEEK_END}}, {{NAME}}. Commands: 'Insert daily dashboard' (inserts daily template at cursor), 'Insert weekly dashboard', 'New Project' (creates project folder note from template, prompts for name). Add template fields to settings tab (daily, weekly, project). Depends on sprint 1 (foundation).
acceptance_criteria: Template commands insert correct content. {{DATE}} resolves to today. New Project creates folder + folder note with frontmatter. Settings tab has editable template fields with reset-to-default button.

title: Sprint 5: Daily/weekly dashboard templates
description: Add task-planner-weekly code block (this week, grouped by project). Add task-planner-project code block (project-scoped tasks). Add checkbox toggle in table (click to mark [x] in source file). Parse + display priority emoji column. Add recurrence parsing (🔁 every day/week/month) with auto-regeneration on completion. Depends on sprint 2 (project engine).
acceptance_criteria: Weekly code block shows tasks grouped by project. Project code block shows tasks scoped to current project. Clicking checkbox in table marks task done in source. Priority column shows emoji. Recurrence generates next task instance.

title: Sprint 4: Status bar timer — persistent running task indicator
description: Add StatusBarItem to Obsidian status bar. Shows running task name + countdown. Click to pause/resume, right-click to stop. Only one timer at a time; starting a new timer stops the previous. Sync with per-row table timer state. Format: ⏱ 23:45 — Task name [⏸]. Depends on sprint 1 (foundation).
acceptance_criteria: Starting a row timer shows status bar timer. Stopping clears it. Click pauses/resumes. Right-click stops. Multiple timers don't stack.

title: Add Today.md — persistent today note at vault root
Add routines section to settings tab
description: UI in FlowtimeSettingsTab with: routines folder path input, vacation mode toggle, auto-generate toggles, workday config, week start day, hide completed routines toggle. Wire all to plugin settings.

title: Add nth-day-of-month and interval-gap recurrence
description: Parse 'every 2nd Sun' (nth weekday of month), 'every month on 15th' (specific date), 'every 3 days' (interval gap from last generation).

title: Settings — Routines Section
description: Add a new Routines section to the settings tab with: routines folder path, vacation mode toggle, auto-generate on startup toggle, auto-generate on daily note open toggle, workday configuration, week start day, and hide completed routines toggle.

title: Vacation Mode
description: Global toggle that pauses all routine generation. Accessible from settings and as a quick-action button in the weekplan toolbar. When active, the routine engine skips all due evaluation and does not write any instances.

title: Styling for inbox modals
description: Add CSS to styles.css for the inbox processing modal. Reuse existing flowtime modal patterns. Style: action dropdown, conditional field groups, editable text area, progress indicator, skip/process/done buttons. Should look consistent with QuickEntryModal and other Flowtime UI.
acceptance_criteria: Processing modal has consistent spacing and colors. Action dropdown and conditional fields are visually grouped. Progress indicator clear. Buttons properly styled. Works in light and dark themes.
close_reason: CSS for inbox processing modal: progress, text area, fields container, labels, input styling

title: Edge cases for inbox
description: Handle edge cases: inbox doesn't exist → offer to create. inbox is empty → show message and exit. All lines discarded → empty file. File modified externally during processing → re-read before write. Line too long → truncate display only. No projects exist → project field shows hint. Snooze date reached → line reappears. Write-back should preserve exact file structure for non-processed lines (headings, blanks, order).
acceptance_criteria: Empty inbox shows message and exits gracefully. Missing inbox offers to create. External edits handled by re-read before write. Long lines truncated in display only. No projects shows helpful message. Snoozed lines filtered until date passes. Non-processed lines/headings preserved exactly.
close_reason: Snoozed items preserved in write-back, concurrent edit handling (re-read before write), long line truncation, empty/missing inbox handling, no-projects error message, write-back preserves headings

title: Canonical workspace layout in skill.md
description: problems.md #1 — Vault structure diagram, key layout rules, dashboard file reference table.
close_reason: Done — Canonical Workspace Layout section in skill.md with vault tree, key rules, dashboard table

title: projectsRoot change detection + daily notes check
description: problems.md #4, #8 — When projectsRoot changes, clear cache + show notice. Check daily-notes.json folder exists on load, warn if missing.
close_reason: Done — projectsRoot change clears cache + shows notice, daily-notes folder checked on load

title: New Project scaffolds all 3 files + no fake tasks

title: Reset to Defaults + Rebuild Cache commands
description: problems.md #1 (Reset) — Add Cmd+P Flowtime: Reset to Defaults (clears all settings + cache) and Flowtime: Rebuild Task Cache commands
close_reason: Done — Reset to Defaults confirmation modal + Rebuild Cache command both implemented

title: Save/Load View uses prompt() — not supported in Obsidian
description: prompt() is not available in Obsidian's Electron renderer. Replace with a Modal-based approach or remove the feature for now.

title: Bump manifest.json to v0.3.0
description: Update manifest.json version from current to 0.3.0. Breaking changes (new syntax, new data model) warrant minor version bump.
acceptance_criteria: manifest.json shows version 0.3.0. Plugin loads correctly in Obsidian.

title: Extract task-parser.js module from renderer.js
description: plan.md §2.1 specified src/task-parser.js. Never extracted. Parsing lives inline in renderer.js loadTasks(). Extract into src/task-parser.js. Needed for Views filter/sort/group pipeline. Format: @directive parser (new syntax).
acceptance_criteria: task-parser.js extracted. All parsing logic moved from renderer.js. Plugin still works identically. New @-prefix syntax parsed correctly.

title: Persist named views
description: Save named view configurations with filter/sort/group/column settings. Re-use across notes. Ships as part of Views Full. Reference: plan_v2.md V5.
acceptance_criteria: Users can save views with a name. Saved views appear in a dropdown or list. Existing flowtime-* code blocks map to built-in presets.

title: Configurable column visibility
description: Configurable column visibility — choose which fields/columns are displayed per view. Ships as part of Views Light (after Sessions). Reference: plan_v2.md V4.
acceptance_criteria: Users can toggle column visibility per view. Settings persist while view is open.

title: Two-level grouping system
description: Two-level grouping (e.g. group by bucket, sub-group by status). Ships as part of Views Full. Reference: plan_v2.md V3.
acceptance_criteria: Grouping works on any field. Two-level grouping works (group + sub-group). Group headers collapse/expand.

title: Multi-column sort system
description: Multi-column sort with priority ordering (e.g. bucket ASC, then priority DESC). Ships as part of Views Full. Reference: plan_v2.md V2.
acceptance_criteria: Users can sort by multiple columns. Sort indicators show direction. Sort persists during session.

title: Custom filter system — recursive AND/OR filter tree
description: Custom filter system with recursive AND/OR filter tree on any field (date, bucket, project, status). Ships as part of Views Full (after Buckets + Sessions data model finalized). Reference: plan_v2.md V1.
acceptance_criteria: Filters can be built with multiple conditions. AND/OR grouping works. Filters correctly narrow task list.

title: Time analytics — derived metrics from sessions
description: Derived metrics from session data: time-by-bucket (per day, per week), weekly totals vs bucket limits, daily streaks, last N completions. Queryable via session-store module. Reference: plan_v2.md S3.
acceptance_criteria: Analytics show time-by-bucket for any day or week. Weekly totals compare against bucket limits. Last N completions queryable.

title: Budget bar overview view
description: New view or section showing all buckets with their progress bars. Overview of all bucket budgets and current usage. Reference: plan_v2.md P4.
acceptance_criteria: Budget overview shows all buckets with progress bars. Daily cap bar shown at top. Each bar shows state color correctly.

title: Add /add-task slash command
description: Register an Obsidian editor suggester that triggers on /add-task in the editor. Selecting it opens the QuickEntryModal. Reference: plan_v2.md Q5, sprint 6 original item that was never implemented.
acceptance_criteria: Typing /add-task in the editor shows a suggester. Selecting it opens the quick entry modal.

title: Write duration to task line on quick-add
description: Quick entry modal has a duration dropdown but explicitly skips writing it to the task line (src/quick-entry.js line 187 comment). Add duration writing: save as @<duration> on the line (e.g. @30m, @1.5h). Reference: plan_v2.md Q4.
acceptance_criteria: Selecting a duration in quick entry writes @<duration> to the task line.

title: Extract status-timer.js module from main.js
description: Timer methods are still in main.js (startStatusTimer, stopStatusTimer, toggleStatusTimer, updateStatusBar). Extract into src/status-timer.js as a StatusTimer class. Wire into plugin onload. Reference: plan_v2.md Q3, original plan.md section 2.1.
acceptance_criteria: All timer logic in src/status-timer.js. Plugin still works identically. Status bar timer + row timer sync unchanged.

title: Reset-to-default buttons on template settings
description: Add small 'Restore default' link next to each template textarea in settings tab. Clicking it replaces current value with the hardcoded default from DEFAULT_SETTINGS. Reference: plan_v2.md Q2, acceptance criteria from original sprint 5b (ewx) that was never fulfilled.
acceptance_criteria: Each template field has a reset link. Clicking restores the shipping default.

title: Add CTAs to empty states
description: Empty states currently show text only ('🎉 No overdue tasks!'). Add actionable CTA buttons: 'Pull tasks from Backlog' (with button linking to backlog view), 'Add a task' (opens quick entry modal), 'Create your first bucket' (if no buckets exist). Reference: plan_v2.md Q1.
acceptance_criteria: Every empty view has a relevant action button. Clicking the button performs the expected action.

title: "Views Are Lenses" Pattern
description:
Custom view system: every view is a configurable lens over the same task data. Split into two phases per review decision:

Views Light (ships after Sessions):
V4 - Configurable column visibility
V6 - Backward compat — existing flowtime-* code blocks become built-in presets

Views Full (after data model finalized — Buckets + Sessions):
V1 - Custom filter system — recursive AND/OR filter tree on any field
V2 - Multi-column sort
V3 - Two-level grouping
V5 - Persist named view configurations

If a view filter references a deleted bucket, show warning but still render.
acceptance_criteria: Views Light: users can configure column visibility per view. Existing flowtime-* blocks still work as presets. Views Full: users can create custom views with filter/sort/group. Views are persisted and reusable.

comments.author: Radialarray
comments.text: Review decisions: Split into Views Light (V4+V6, ships after Sessions) and Views Full (V1-V3+V5, after Buckets+Sessions data model finalized). Deleted bucket reference shows warning, not silent skip.

title: Quick Win Polish
description: Small standalone improvements that deliver high UX signal for minimal effort. Empty state CTAs, reset-to-default buttons, status-timer module extraction, duration writing on quick-add (@<duration> format), /add-task slash command, task-parser.js module extraction from renderer.js.
acceptance_criteria: All 6 items implemented and verified.

comments.author: Radialarray
comments.text: Review decisions: Q4 uses new @<duration> format (e.g. @1.5h, @30m). Q6 added — extract task-parser.js. Q3 + Q6 refactoring enables Views pipeline.

comments.author: Radialarray
comments.text: Wave 1 done: Q2 (reset buttons) + Q3 (status-timer.js) + Q4 (duration write) + Q6 (task-parser.js). Wave 2: Q5 (slash command) + Q1 (empty state CTAs) in progress.

title: Sprint 4b: Configurable notifications — Notice API settings
description: Add notification settings to settings tab. Controls: timerSound (toggle beep on expiry), noticeDuration (ms, default 4000, 0 = persistent), quietMode (suppress all non-error notices). Wire into existing timer expiry, task completion, bulk action, and error notification paths. Use Obsidian Notice API throughout. Depends on sprint 1 (foundation).
acceptance_criteria: Settings tab has notification controls. Toggling timerSound off mutes beep. quietMode suppresses non-error notices. noticeDuration controls all Notice display times.

title: Sprint 6: Polish — hotkeys, slash command, theme compat
description: Add Cmd+Shift+T hotkey for quick entry modal. Add /add-task slash command in editor. Performance: incremental task parsing. Theme compatibility fixes. Depends on sprints 1-5.
acceptance_criteria: Hotkey opens quick entry. Slash command works in editor. No visual glitches in default + community themes.

title: Skill.md updates + @due: docs + troubleshooting
description: problems.md #9, #10.2, #10.3 — Status field docs ('' vs ' '), @due: syntax docs, troubleshooting section for cache/sessions
close_reason: Done — status field docs corrected, @due: syntax added, troubleshooting section added

title: Weekplan View — Horizontal Timeline Grid
description: Phase 2 enhancement to the weekplan view. A proper horizontal week grid with time slots on the left, days across the top, and task cells spanning their time range. Drag-to-resize, click-to-edit. Routines shown with subtle border/label.
